info.title: ZMS Backend (human waiting queue management) API
info.description:
The ZMS system is intended to manage human waiting queues. It has the following features:

 * make appointments via a calender and initiate a process to manage an appointment
 * import requests (services) and providers (locations) from external sources
 * manage scopes for appointments, including a four level hierarchy of owner->organisation->department->scope
 * manage opening hours including closed days
 * login-system with different access levels
 * ticketprinter support for customers without appointments (authenticated, lockable, timeable)
 * calldisplay support
 * collecting statistics like waiting time or served clients per day
 * emergency call for employees

 This documentation covers the API-level access to these features.

 ## Common parameters for requests

 ### Header X-JsonCompressLevel

 Setting the header "X-JsonCompressLevel" with a value of 1 or higher enables a json schema based compression. On a value of 1 and higher, default value for properties are omitted. To have a combine the default values in the entity schema with the response. With a value of 2 and higher (not implemented yet), repeating values are referenced inline with a "$ref" property. A JSON-Schema dereferencing is required.

 This header is useful on results with a lot of items.

 ### Parameter resolveReferences

 Most calls support the parameter "resolveReferences". Usually by fetching an entity, it includes an amount of sub entities. For example, a department has a list of scopes. Fetching a department usually does not include the scopes. If you need the scopes of the department, use the parameter "resolveReferences" with a value of 1. This resolves one level of sub entities. If you need for example the availabilities (opening hours) of the scopes, use a value of 2 to fetch two sublevels of entities. Beware, that a high value results in a lot of data which might be redundant.

 Why using a parameter like this and not different api calls? The overhead for an api call is usually on around 20ms. Fetching a list for different entities can easily result in 20 additional requests to fetch the required data. So using this parameter might result having a 10 times higher performance compared to mulitple calls.

 ### Experimental GraphQL parameter

 There is an experimental "gql" parameter to reduce the download size of fetched data. Sometimes only a part of the data is required for the desired operation. This parameter supports a simplified GraphQL query. It is experimental in a sense, that the amount of the supported GraphQL features might change.

 Currently only the selection of desired fields are supported. To get a scope with only the ID and the name, a query like this accomplishes this.

 `/api/2/scope/141/?gql={id+contact{name}}`

 As difference to the GraphQL implementation, a whole sub section might be retrieved by not adding details. But beware of using this, for a stricter GraphQL implementation in the future might break this behavior. To get all contents of `scope.contact` a query like this currently solves this.

 `/api/2/scope/141/?gql={id+contact}`

 The API is currently not GraphQL compatible. A request does not get faster on the server for requesting not every property. But there might be a performance increase on the client side for there is less data to download and decode.

httpauth.description:
The API uses two variants of authentication. An application can use basic authentication via HTTP for identification. As an alternative, there is a header with the name "X-Authkey". This key can be optained by accessing "/workstation/login/".

According to the application settings, there might be different rights to use API methods. Further rights depend on the settings for the authorized useraccount.

tags.name: apikey
tags.description:
Über einen "apikey" erhält man Zugriff zu der Terminbuchung. Die Eigenschaft "key" wird für die meisten HTTP-Requests als Header `X-Auth-Key` benötigt.

Eine Verifikation für einen apikey ist notwendig. Dies erfolgt in der Regel über ein Captcha. Über ein "clientkey" kann man aber auch erweiterte Berechtigungen erhalten, wie zum Beispiel Zugriff auf begrenzte Terminkontingente oder einen Verzicht auf das Captcha. Der "clientkey" wird vom Betreiber des Systems mitgeteilt. Ohne einen solchen "clientkey" werden Standard-Einstellungen verwendet.

Weiterhin gehört zu einem apikey ein Quota für unterschiedliche Requests. Um Missbrauch der Schnittstelle zu vermeiden werden in unterschiedlichen Zeiteinheiten nur eine begrenzte Zahl von Requests zugelassen. Eine Information wieviele Requests noch frei sind, erhält man über den apikey.

tags.name: calendar
tags.description:
Ein "calendar" repräsentiert eine Kalender-Ansicht. Diese beinhaltet zum einen die Details der Abfrage, wie für welchen Zeitraum der Kalender gilt und welche Leistungen and welchen Standorten buchbar sind. Als Antwort auf eine Anfrage enthält der Kalender Einträge zu Tagen, an denen Termine buchbar sind.

Wichtig: Der "calendar" repräsentiert keinen Kalender eines Standortes oder ähnliches. Es handelt sich um ein temporäres Suchobjekt mit Suchergebnissen.

tags.name: process
tags.description:
Unter "process" wird ein Vorgang im System verstanden. Ein solcher Vorgang enthält in der Regel einen Termin sowie Kontaktdaten zum Kunden und Details zum Standort des Termins.

Ein "process" kann auch einen freien Termin repräsentieren. Dies lässt sich über die Eigenschaft `status` erkennen. Ein Status "free" bedeutet, dass es sich um einen freien Termin handelt, ein "reserved" kennzeichnet eine flüchtige Reservierung und ein Vorgang im Status "confirmed" entspricht einem gültigen Termin.

tags.name: provider
tags.description: Ein "provider" entspricht einem physischen Standort mit Ortsdaten wie Straße und Hausnummer und häufig Angaben, wo im entsprechenden Gebäude dieser Dienstleister zu finden ist. Der "provider" legt fest, welche Leistungen ("request") an einem "scope" verfügbar sind. Ein "provider" kann mehrere zugehörige "scope"-Objekte haben.

tags.name: scope
tags.description: Neben dem "provider" gibt es einen "scope". Dieser "scope" umfasst vor allem einem Terminkalender und daneben einen Warteraum (oder auch Aufrufanlage, "calldisplay") und Arbeitsplätze ("workstation"). Wenn mehrere "scope"-Objekte sich einen Warteraum ("calldisplay") und Arbeitsplätze ("workstation") teilen, können diese von einem "cluster" zusammengefasst werden. Dabei müssen die "scope" im Falle eines "cluster" nicht zum selben "provider" gehören.

apikey.description: Representation of an Apikey. It usually stores temporary api quota data while appointment scheduling in service app

route.description: path of an URL for which the quota is valid

period.description: Period of time for this quota

ts.description: unix timestamp representing the creating time of quota
unix timestamp representing the creating time of quota

apiclient.description: An apiclient is required on requesting an apikey.

apiClientID.description: internal ID for the API client

accesslevel.description: access level of the api key

lastChange.description: unix timestamp representing the creating time of quota

appointment.description: An appointment represents a given time on a scope for defining appointments in a calendar.

date.description: unix timestamp representing the date and time of the appointment

availability.description: An Availability is a representation of opening hours for a scope. It defines the time a scope offers appointments and a ticket printer is able to serve process numbers.

startInDays.description: number of days relative to today to start offering appointments

endInDays.description: maximum number of days in the future to offer appointments

endDate.description: unix timestamp of a day until this availability is valid

id.description: ID for the availability. A NULL or zero means, that the availability is new.

kind.description: The kind of availability, used for validation and filtering

lastChange.description: unix timestamp of the last change on this availability

multipleSlotsAllowed.description: an appointment is allowed to take more than one time slot

items.description: Non persistent note usable for processing or logging calculation results, usually used for debugging

afterWeeks.description: number of weeks for repeating the times, usually 1, 2 or 3

weekOfMonth.description: number of week in month, on a value of 5 the last week of the month, even it is the 4th

slotTimeInMinutes.description: Multiplicated with a slotCount from an appointment this gives the duration in minutes for a appointment/process.

startDate.description: unix timestamp of a day to start offering appointments

type.description:
The following type are available
* appointment - defines times for appointments
* openinghours - defines times where a scope is opened
* break - defines times for a break (not implemented yet)

intern.description: maximum of workstations expected to handle appointments

public.description: number of workstations to offer appointments for booking using a public website

calendar.description: Represents a calendar showing free days for appointments using a list of clusters, providers and scopes. Usually the list of clusters, providers are converted to a list of scopes. If all these lists are empty, the list of scopes is generated by available scopes for the given requests.

bookableEnd.description: unix timestamp of a day representing the end of the bookable time. Paging after thise date should reveal no new appointments.

calldisplay.description:
A calldisplay is a device to call people in a waiting room to a workstation. Usually it is a display on wall showing process ids together with a room number.
The clusters are resolved to fill the list of scopes. The text displayed on the calldisplay depends on the given scopes. If only one scope is present, a text can be displayed on the display. On multiple scopes, no text and no image is displayed.

image.description: A symbolic image for the scope

mime.description: mime type of the base64 encoded image

serverTime.description: unix timestamp of the server time to adjust the clock on the call display

client.description: A client is usually a customer in a scope who intends to get his request solved. Usually the client has an appointment, if not he got a process number from a ticket printer and his familyName is set to 'Spontankunde'.

surveyAccepted.description: true if clients allows survey requests

cluster.description: A cluster is a way to group different scopes.

example.callDisplayText:
Herzlich Willkommen,
Die aufgerufenen Plätze finden Sie den im Gang rechts.
example.name: Bürger- und Standesamt

callDisplayText.description: text for the call display if only the cluster is selected

hint.description: information for the client like the address to a cluster location

shortNameEnabled.description: true if the scope short name should be display next to the workstation name

emergency.description: Depending on scope settings, a workstation might have an emergency-button to call for help

refreshInterval.description: default time in seconds for next check if an emergency occurred

contact.description: A contact is a collection of contact informations to find or to contact a natural person or an entity.

country.description: Name of the country or the ISO 3166-1 country code like 'de'.

lat.description: Latitute of a geo coordinate as wgs84 or etrs89

lon.description: Longitude of a geo coordinate as wgs84 or etrs89

messages.pattern: Die E-Mail Adresse muss eine valide E-Mail im Format max@mustermann.de sein

day.description: Representation of a day in the calendar. You might find curious values in freeAppointments. While public values might be positive, the intern value can be negative. This is due to calculation. The public values are calculated per slot whereas the intern value is calculated for the whole day/all summed scopes. If the intern value is lower 0 and beyond, some slots might be booked with more clients than available. This might be due to changes in the availability settings.

processList.description: list of processes, only available if status is detail

status.description:
Status of day. The following values are possible:
  * bookable: day has free appointments
  * notBookable: day is not bookable because of missing availabilities
  * restricted: not bookable because of dayoff times
  * full: not bookable because missing free appointments
  * detail: non calculated status, every process for the day is available unter processList (very expansive in resources, use with caution)

department.description: A department is a subunit in an organisation like "Bürgeramt", "Standesamt", "Vertrieb".

email.description: sender address for reminder mails to the clients

messages.pattern: Die E-Mail Adresse muss eine valide E-Mail im Format max@mustermann.de sein

sendEmailReminderMinutesBefore.description: number of minutes before appointment when reminder e-mail is sent

dayoff.description: A dayoff is a free day where a department and its scopes are closed. Beware, if an availability is valid only for one day, it overrules a dayoff, so you can administrate exceptions on a particular scope.

lastChange.description: unix timestamp of the last change on this dayoff

exchange.description: Represents an exchanged set of data usuable for statistics. It can include information how the data is changed.

period.description: Type of periodic granularity of data.

position.description: Each row of data is a list of values. The position indicated whicht element in the list is refered by this dictionary entry. Counting begins with zero.

variable.description: Name of the variable for the given position

description.description: A detailed description explaining the variable and contraints on interpreting the variable.

reference.description: A reference to another entity to obtain more informations.

items.description: For a description on the values, see the 'dictionary'. Non existing

title.description: Title for the data, might include hints for the data

visualization.description: Hints for visualization of the data

target.description: true if url should open in a new window

public.description: true if url should displayed as public link

organisation.description: greater than 0 if link is available for given organisation

type.description: Type of log entry to group different entries. According to the type of the log entry, the field reference might have a different meaning

reference.description: Usually an ID according to the type field

ts.description: Unix timestamp marking the time the log entry was written into the database

scope_id.description: Scope for which is log created

mail.description: A mail represents a message to be send to a client and may have different content-types like in a multipart email.

createTimestamp.description: unix timestamp representing creation of the notification

mimepart.description: A message part to send. For images an other binary files, the content can be encoded as base64.

queueId.description: optional ID of queued message item, this part belongs to

mime.description: The mime type used in 'Content-Type', should include charset if necessary. If no charset is given, utf-8 is expected.

base64.description: True if the content is encoded as base64.

metaresult.description: The meta result is used in HTTP-API-requests and has only debugging purposes.

error.description: true if request was not successful

generated.description: iso8601 date time string of generation on the server

length.description: number of items in the data block

message.description: contains additional information if property error is true

retryAfter.description: contains retry after information on status code 429

reducedData.description: has reduced data, do not use for saving data

organisation.description: An organisation is the top unit to group departments with their scopes.

ticketPrinterActivation.description: true if kiosks must be explicitly activated before use

owner.description:
An owner is usually a contract partner using the ZMS. The base scopes of the ZMS are organised in an hierarchy, with the Owner on top. The hierarchy looks like this:
1. owner
2. organisation (by law or area)
3. department (by solving different requests)
4. scope (by building or location)
5. workstation (by person or room)

process.description: A process represents a request from one or more clients, usually including an appointment. Compared to an appointment, the process has additional information about the status of the request from the client.

requirements.name: Abmeldung bei der Meldebehörde

requirements.description:
Seit dem 1. November 2015 ist der Wohnungsgeber verpflichtet, dem Meldepflichtigen den Auszug bis zu 7 Tage vor oder nach dem Auszug schriftlich mit Unterschrift zu bestätigen. Die Bestätigung muss folgende Daten enthalten: Name und Anschrift des Wohnungsgebers, Auszugsdatum, Anschrift der Wohnung und Namen der meldepflichtigen Personen.
Ein Muster für die Auszugsbestätigung des Wohnungsgebers steht Ihnen unter "Formulare" zur Verfügung.
requirements.name: Auszugsbestätigung des Wohnungsgebers (Vermieter)

data.responsibility: Diese Dienstleistung erhalten Sie in allen Berliner Bürgerämtern.
data.fees: gebührenfrei; dies gilt auch für die Abmeldebestätigung.
data.description:
• Sie geben eine Berliner Wohnung auf, weil Sie in das Ausland verziehen

oder
  • Sie geben eine von mehreren Wohnungen in Deutschland auf, für die Sie angemeldet sind und die Berliner Wohnung bleibt bestehen
oder
  • Sie geben eine von mehreren Berliner Wohnungen auf, für die Sie angemeldet sind

Sie müssen sich bei der Meldebehörde in Berlin nur bei einem Wegzug ins Ausland abmelden oder bei der Aufgabe einer Haupt- oder Nebenwohnung, ohne dass Sie in eine andere Wohnung im Inland einziehen.
Beim Wegzug in das Ausland gilt, dass Sie aus dieser Wohnung ausziehen und dort zukünftig nicht wieder wohnen werden - befristete Auslandsaufenthalte, z.B. zu Studienzwecken bedingen keine Abmeldung.


Die Abmeldung kann im Zeitraum von 7 Tagen vor, muss aber bis 14 Tage nach dem Auszug vorgenommen werden.
Sie erhalten eine Abmeldebestätigung.

Hinweis:
Wenn Sie jedoch innerhalb Deutschlands nur umziehen wollen, brauchen Sie sich lediglich bei der Meldebehörde Ihrer neuen Wohnung anmelden.

displayNumber.description: Appointment number that should be displayed

amendment.description: additional information for this process from the client

customTextfield.description: additional textfield for this process from the client

customTextfield2.description: second textfield for this process from the client

archiveId.description: Number is used to identify a process in an archive (for statistical purposes)

authKey.description: key for client to delete or change process

priority.description: Process priority number. 1 is biggest prio, 3 is lowest

createTimestamp.description: unix timestamp representing creation of the process

id.description: Number is used to identify appointments like on call displays

lastChange.description: unix timestamp of the last change on this process

wasMissed.description: Indicates whether the process was marked as missed at any prior point.

showUpTime.description: unix timestamp of showing up after call

processingTime.description: Cumulative time spent being served at the workstation (from show-up until finish or timeout), summed across interrupted sessions. MySQL TIME string (H+:MM:SS) from persistence, or fractional minutes as number; null if unset—not a Unix timestamp

timeoutTime.description: unix timestamp of after a call is entered or re-entered into the queue

finishTime.description: unix timestamp og time when process has been finished

status.description:
Status of the process. The following values are possible:
  * free: open for reservation
  * reserved: for updating data, no validation yet
  * confirmed: appointment
  * queued: confirmed and in waiting queue
  * called: show on call displays
  * processing: client appeared and gets service
  * pending: client has to return to fetch papers
  * finished: successfully processed
  * missed: no client appeared
  * archived: only used in statistics
  * deleted: blocking appointment time
  * anonymized: for statistically purposes only
  * blocked: to avoid the reuse of IDs on the same day
  * conflict: mark process as conflict for check functions

externalUserId.description: External user identifier for linking process to authenticated citizen user

provider.description: A provider usually represents an organisational entity offering services to clients. In most cases, it is a building or a special location hosting an organisation which provides the services. A service is represented by a request in the ZMS.

link.description: URI with detailed information for the provider

name.description: name of the referenced service (explicit redundant)

source.description: source of provider to resolve reference id

queue.description: A queue represents a waiting queue for processes/clients.

arrivalTime.description: unix timestamp when client was added to the queue or appointment time

callCount.description: number of calls for the client to appear at the workstation

destination.description: Text to display on calling the queued item, usually a room number.

destinationHint.description: Small hint to display on calling the queued item.

lastCallTime.description: unix timestamp of last call while client not appeared and process was not stopped

number.description: number to display on a call display

displayNumber.description: number to display on a call display

waitingTime.description: Time spent waiting until the client is called: from the booked appointment start (e.g. appointment 12:00, called 12:03 → 3 minutes) or from joining the walk-in queue. A duration—fractional minutes (number) or MySQL TIME string (H+:MM:SS)—not a clock time

wayTime.description: Time from call to arrival at desk: fractional minutes (number) or MySQL TIME string (H+:MM:SS)

waitingTimeEstimate.description: estimated time in minutes to wait for a call

waitingTimeOptimistic.description: estimated time in minutes to wait for a call (optimistic value for views with timespan)

withAppointment.description: true if corresponding process has an appointment

status.description:
Status of the process. The following values are possible:
  * confirmed: confirmed appointment but not in queue
 * queued: confirmed and in waiting queue
 * blocked: to avoid the reuse of IDs on the same day
 * called: show on call displays
  * deleted: client is deleted
  * processing: client appeared and gets service
  * pending: client has to return for getting papers
  * reserved: appointment is reserved
  * finished: successfully processed
  * fake: dummy entry for getting infos about queue
  * missed: no client appeared

request.description: A request is usually a representation of a service offered by a provider.

emergency.description: If activated, a workstation has an emergency-button to call for help

acceptedByWorkstation.description: name of workstation which accepted the emergency call

calledByWorkstation.description: name of workstation where the emergency occured

givenNumberCount.description: counter for given waiting numbers for the current day

workstationCount.description: a calculated number of workstations to calculate waiting times, if ghostWorkstationCount < 1, real logged in workstations are used

lastGivenNumberTimestamp.description: unix timestamp of the last given waiting number

deactivated.description: true if all ticket printers are disabled

session.description: Representation of a session. It usually stores temporary appointment data before a free appointment is found.

entry.description: Save request IDs and provider IDs from entry query

status.description:
Status of the session. The following values are possible:
* start: new session started
* reserved: set if a process has been reserved
* confirmed: set if a process has been confirmed
* finished: set if a process has been finished
* inChange: current process is in change mode
* changedProcess: current process has been changed
* stalled: set if session has a problem, fore example if second tab is in use 
* inProgress: if session not stalled anymore

error.description: error or notice from errorhandler to show specific template message block

slot.description: This entity represents a calculated number for available appointments. Attention: Be aware that the different types of appointments are not exclusive, the total amount of appointments are not equal to the sum of appointments from the different types.

intern.description: Appointments of this type are usually only available for authenticated workstations

public.description: Appointments of this type are available for everyone

slotnumber.description: This field is set, if the slot is part of a incrementing list of slots

time.description: Format is 12:34 with HH:mm without seconds. This field is set, if the slot represents a time of a day

type.description:
Type of the process. The following values are possible:
  * free: the values represent possible free appointments without confirmed appointments
  * timeslice: the values represent free appointments for a given day. Confirmed and reserved appointments on processes are substracted.
  * sum: like timeslice, but for more than one scope
  * reduced: like timeslice, but numbers were reduced due to required slots on a given request
  * timestamp: the values represent a unix timestamp to when there are free processes

source.description: An source represents an import from a data source providing data for providers and requests as well as their relations.

editable.description: if this is true, a user might upload an update of the source, on false, this source is updated via automated scripts

lastChange.description: unix timestamp of the last import

status.description: A list of status informations for the API and the application.

generated.description: iso8601 date time string of generation on the server

blocked.description: Number of temporary blocked appointment IDs in the database.

called.description: Number of processes with status called (displayed on call displays, not yet in processing).

confirmed.description: Number of valid appointments in the database. Every process counts for the clients involved.

deleted.description: Number of deleted appointments in the database blocking free appointment times.

lastCalculate.description: Timestamp of last calculation for slots

missed.description: Number of missed appointments in the database. Zeroed on daily basis in the night.

parked.description: Number of parked appointments in the database. Zeroed on daily basis in the night.

outdated.description: Number of slots, which might change on recalculation due to changed availabilities

outdatedOldest.description: Timestamp of oldest slot for recalculation.

reserved.description: Number of reserved appointments in the database.

withExternalUserId.description: Number of processes with externalUserId set (buerger.external_user_id is not null and not empty), any status.

confirmedWithExternalUserId.description: Number of confirmed processes with externalUserId set (OIDC-linked appointments).

last.description: Last date(c) of dldb import in database.

queueCount.description: Number of unsend items in the queue

oldestSeconds.description: Unix timestamp of oldest unsend item in queue

newestSeconds.description: Unix timestamp of newest unsend item in queue

locks.description: Number of locks for transactions on requested node.

nodeConnections.description: Percentage of used connections on requested node.

problems.description: List of configuration problems on requested node..

threads.description: Number of connected threads on requested node.

major.description: major version, this value increments on breaking changes

minor.description: minor version, this value increments on adding features

patch.description: patch level, this value increments with bugfixes

ticketprinter.description: A ticketprinter is a system to print waiting numbers for clients. Usually it has a touch screen and a printing device.

name.description: Name of the Button, shown value

type.description: Type of the button, for complement links

enabled.description: To enable or disable button usage

url.description: url to scope, cluster or external target

buttonlist.description: comma separated list of scope, cluster, links (s = scope, c = cluster, l = link), max of 6 buttons

enabled.description: true if browser is allowed to hand out waiting numbers to the client

hash.description: identifier for a browser (usually cookie content)

lastUpdate.description: unix timestamp of last status change

reload.description: time to redirect to homepage in seconds
